_doc: Paradata registry (London Charter / Seville Principles): the reasoning behind modeled or inferred choices. Facts reference these by id in `paradataRef` instead of repeating prose.

pd-fig-6-4-status.rationale: fig.6-4 ('Schematic section through the qanat system showing the status of the present research', H. Werner) codes each stretch of the route's bottom bar black (explored/walked) or grey (unexplored), and each shaft's own vertical body the same way; a separate red square marks construction work at the shaft head, filled (done) or hollow (not done/planned) — some shafts carry no marker at all. Digitised by eye from a high-resolution scan (pixel-level colour sampling against a coordinate grid, not a casual read) after an earlier pass under-read the pattern as a single explored/unexplored split. CORRECTED READING (2026-07-22): the gallery bar actually alternates TWICE — grey P-5→P-1, black P-1→P2, grey P2→P4, black P4→P9 — not once as first modelled; the P2→P4 grey stretch (shafts P2 and P3 themselves) was missed entirely in the first pass. One break (between P-4 and P-1) falls partway along that span, not on a shaft, so it is grouped with the nearer shaft (P-1) rather than invented as a mid-span coordinate. Construction-work markers: filled/done at P-7A, P-5A, P-4, P1, P4, P5, P6, P7, P8, P9; hollow/not-done at P-5; NO marker at all at P-1, P0, P2, P3 (read as 'not applicable', not 'not done'). SHAFT-BODY colour (added 2026-07-22, initially missed — the first pass only carried the gallery-bar and marker semantics, not each shaft's own black/grey body, which a user comparison against the source caught): every shaft body is ALSO independently drawn black (explored) or grey (unexplored), and this does NOT simply mirror which gallery-bar segment the shaft sits in — e.g. P-4's body is black even though it sits in the grey P-5→P-1 gallery segment, and P4's body is grey even though it sits in the black P4→P9 gallery segment. Read as two distinct facts (has the SHAFT itself been explored, vs. has the TUNNEL reaching it been walked), both digitised and both modelled: explored/black at P-7A, P-5A, P-4, P1, P5, P6, P7, P8, P9; unexplored/grey at P-1, P0, P2, P3, P4. P-1 and P4 are each drawn with a short black 'explored' segment starting at the surface, above an otherwise-grey body — modelled (2026-07-22) as a real excavated depth (shaftCollarDepthM), NOT a cosmetic override on the app's own above-ground cap stub (an earlier pass modelled it that way; corrected on request since the app's cap segment is a fixed schematic convention unrelated to what the figure is actually depicting). METHOD: measured each shaft's black-segment and total-bar pixel heights directly off a high-resolution scan of fig.6-4 (P-1: ~35 px black of ~200 px total; P4: ~50 px black of ~380 px total), then calibrated a pixels-per-metre scale using the three main-line shafts in this SAME figure with an independently documented real depth elsewhere in this dataset (P1=30 m → 10.83 px/m; P3=35 m → 11.14 px/m; P5=36 m → 10.0 px/m — a ~10% internal spread). Applying that ~10.7 px/m average: P-1's total bar implies ~19 m (giving a ~3 m black segment) and P4's total bar implies ~35 m (giving a ~5 m black segment). CAVEAT: a fourth calibration shaft, P-4 (12 m documented), gave a very different scale (15.4 px/m) — fig.6-4 states its own caption is 'schematic', so pixel height is evidently NOT linearly proportional to real depth across its full range; the calibration is only reasonably trustworthy near the 30-36 m band the three anchors span. P4's implied ~35 m sits inside that band (confidence: medium); P-1's implied ~19 m sits in the uncalibrated gap between the shallow auxiliary shafts (6-8 m) and the deep main shafts (30-36+ m) (confidence: low) — both depths are order-of-magnitude estimates, not measurements, and should not be cited as documented figures. P-5 is the only shaft whose whole body is drawn in the construction-work red rather than black/grey — modelled as its own third shaft-body state, distinct from the explored/unexplored pair. P-5's hollow marker sits directly beside the figure's 'channel enclosure and visitor's gallery' (b) annotation, itself also drawn hollow, so the two most plausibly describe the same not-yet-built feature. P1 additionally carries a second red mark and a small black flag/arrow icon partway down its shaft — not modelled (outside the explored/unexplored/construction-work legend the toggle visualises; likely related to the documented P1 sounding-gallery/spring-capture feature, already modelled separately — see pd-p1-sounding-gallery). The lateral channel (a) and the visitor's-gallery enclosure (b) themselves are NOT modelled as positioned 3D geometry; their exact position/extent isn't recoverable from this schematic alone.
pd-fig-6-4-status.author: project team

pd-gallery-gradient.rationale: The gallery is modeled near-level at ~0.1% (10 cm per 100 m), the documented constant fall from P9 to P-5, with the two documented stepped falls (1 m under P4, 1.2 m under P6). Step magnitudes are ~1 m and therefore sub-visible at scene scale, so they are shown as annotated markers rather than literal geometry.
pd-gallery-gradient.author: project team

pd-schematic-positions.rationale: Per-shaft plan positions (modelPos) are read as relative along-route spacing from fig.3-1 (the 3D block model, which carries a 100 m scale bar) and cross-checked against fig.5-3 (schematic longitudinal section) and the documented inter-shaft distances (see gallery.documentedDistances). The along-route scale is calibrated at ~7 m per model x-unit via the documented P2–P3 = 28 m (modelPos x 4→8). Under that scale the auxiliary shafts P-5A/P-7A were re-spaced (2026-07-02) so P-7A sits ~25 m NW of P-5 as documented (previously ~45 m — the over-spacing flagged in review). fig.3-1 is an oblique 3D perspective, so spacing elsewhere is approximate, NOT georeferenced. Positions are declared in a `model-schematic` CRS; real WGS84 coordinates (geo) are left null (see pd-source-precedence). Correction: these positions were previously mis-cited to fig.5-4, which is actually a photograph of shaft P2.
pd-schematic-positions.basis: Brochure 2018, fig.3-1 (p.7, 100 m scale bar); fig.5-3 (p.17); documented distances p.17, p.22
pd-schematic-positions.author: project team

pd-source-precedence.rationale: Source-of-truth hierarchy for this model: (1) the official SIT brochure (Waringo/Faber/Werner 2018) is authoritative for ALL qanat facts — shaft depths, surveyed floor elevations, gallery gradient and steps, geology, hydrology, and the route/spacing arrangement; (2) EU-DEM 25 m (OpenTopoData) is external CONTEXT terrain only — it supplies the surrounding landscape surface, not the qanat, and where it disagrees with the brochure the brochure wins; (3) OpenStreetMap shaft nodes are the lowest-authority, provisional cross-check. Critically, the brochure contains NO coordinate grid on any figure (fig.3-1 has only a 100 m scale bar on an oblique 3D view; fig.5-3 is explicitly exaggerated), so true WGS84 georeferencing CANNOT be derived from it. Therefore per-shaft `geo` stays null and OSM remains the only (provisional) georeference candidate — see pd-osm-georef. This is why the EU-DEM surface at OSM points does not reconcile with the brochure's documented depths: they are different, unmerged sources at different authority levels, not an error to 'fix' in the qanat data.
pd-source-precedence.author: project team

pd-postulated-shafts.rationale: Shafts P-2, P-3 and P-6A are inferred from the regular inter-shaft spacing of the located shafts (in qanats the spacing often matches the depth). They are not excavated or surveyed; existence is `inferred`, position `hypothetical` and schematic.
pd-postulated-shafts.author: project team

pd-nominal-datum.rationale: Vertical authority is the brochure: the surveyed shaft-floor elevations (P-7A 354.3, P-5A 357.5, P-4 355.4 m a.s.l.) and the documented depths are the true datum for the qanat. For non-GeoData terrain modes there is no real surface datum, so a nominal site datum is used (valley ~340 m, plateau ~385 m a.s.l.) purely to place the gallery/shafts at plausible depths. GeoData mode draws the SURFACE from EU-DEM metres (context), but the qanat depths/floors remain those documented by the brochure (see pd-source-precedence).
pd-nominal-datum.basis: Brochure 2018 (floors/depths); nominal surface consistent with Brochure 2018 elevations 350-405 m a.s.l.
pd-nominal-datum.author: project team

pd-osm-georef.rationale: Real shaft coordinates were imported from OpenStreetMap (data/osm-shafts.json, source osm-qanat-shafts) as candidate georeferencing. The OSM nodes are named on a '-6..9' scheme that does NOT map 1:1 to the brochure P-labels (P-7A/P-5A/P-5/P-4/P-1/P0/P1..P9), and the OSM 'P2'->'P3' spacing reads ~20 m against a documented 28 m — so the label assignment needed reconciliation. RECONCILIATION 2026-07-01 (method): (1) fig.3-1 (p.7, oblique 3D block model, the only positional figure, carrying a 100 m scale bar) was digitised — the 15 red shaft poles' base x/y pixel positions were detected and the scale bar measured at 311 px = 100 m (0.322 m/px in the foreground plane), giving along-route inter-shaft distances; (2) these were compared with the OSM chain (haversine between consecutive nodes) and the documented distances (P2-P3 = 28 m p.17; P-5->P-7A ~25 m NW p.22), and a similarity fit (scale+offset) was run mapping the OSM cumulative chain onto the digitised cumulative positions to expose residuals / off-by-one shifts; (3) the 0.5 m ACT LiDAR 2019 surface at each OSM point was checked against fig.4-2's W->E surface shape. FINDINGS: The off-by-one test is decisive — aligning the 12 OSM-mapped nodes to the CURRENT labels (P-4..P9) gives RMS 5.8 m, vs 10.8-13.9 m for any ±1/±2/±3 shift, so the OVERALL mapping is correct and there is no systematic label offset. Per-leg (digitised fig.3-1 | OSM | documented): P2->P3 24.4 | 19.8 | 28 m; P3->P4 32.2 | 35.7; P4->P5 32.0 | 31.7; P5->P6 23.5 | 23.7; P6->P7 16.4 | 16.4; P7->P8 20.1 | 20.5; P8->P9 21.3 | 22.3; P-5->P-4 12.3 | 17.9; P-4->P-1 41 | 57.4; P-1->P0 14.0 | 19.1; P0->P1 11.3 | 15.6; P1->P2 14.3 | 23.5. LiDAR profile W->E (366.8, 365.5, 365.2, 366.9, 374.2, 379.4, 386.1, 391.8, 392.5, 391.7, 392.1, 382.1, 378.2, 377.1, 377.2 m) reproduces fig.4-2 exactly: Alzette hillside ~365, monotonic rise to the flat Pëtschend plateau top (P2-P5 ~392), sharp -10 m drop at P6 into the Haedchen depression (P7-P9 ~377) — no elevation outlier signals a mis-label. CONFIDENCE TIERS: HIGH = P3, P4, P5 (plateau top: digitised, OSM and documented spacings all consistent, small fit residuals, flat-plateau LiDAR) and P6, P7, P8, P9 (Haedchen: digitised and OSM inter-shaft distances agree to ~1 m and LiDAR captures the drop — though this is the FAR/EAST end of the oblique figure where perspective foreshortening is worst, so their agreement, not the absolute digitised metres, is what carries the confidence). MEDIUM = P-4, P-1, P0, P1 (route-bend / plateau-edge zone: labels corroborated by LiDAR and sequence but the oblique foreshortening and the long P-4->P-1 leg leave spacing uncertain; P-4 is the single largest fit residual, +12.7 m). LOW = P2 (CONFLICT: OSM makes P1->P2 = 23.5 m the LONGER leg while the brochure says P2 is 'unusually close to P1' p.16-17, and OSM P2->P3 = 19.8 m is well short of the documented 28 m; P2 is the largest negative fit residual, -11 m — the OSM 'P2' node reads ~1/3 too far east) and P-7A, P-5A, P-5 (west-end auxiliary branch: P-7A/P-5A are DERIVED offsets, not OSM nodes, and P-5 rests on the OSM '-5' node while the two westernmost OSM nodes '-6'/'-5' carry no independent label, mapsTo=null; the main line and the separate ~25-30 m northern auxiliary channel per fig.5-14 p.22 cannot be disentangled from OSM alone). CAVEAT: fig.3-1 is an oblique 3D perspective, so all digitised metres are APPROXIMATE and increasingly foreshortened toward the east; they support/refute labels relatively but are not a georeference. LiDAR-at-OSM still reproduces the brochure floor+depth surfaces to 0.5-2.5 m and resolves the Haedchen drop, so the coordinates remain the best provisional georeference; the earlier EU-DEM mismatch (+10-21 m) was a 25 m plateau-edge averaging artifact. NO coordinates were re-mapped in this pass (no unambiguous alternative for the low-confidence nodes) — they are flagged only. RECOMMENDATION: a definitive fix (especially for P2 and the west-end auxiliary shafts) requires the certified SIT/CNRA survey plan the community OSM trace was drawn from (agreed plan step 4, out of scope here).
pd-osm-georef.basis: OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL); Brochure 2018 fig.3-1 (p.7, 100 m scale bar) digitised; documented distances p.17, p.22; fig.4-2 (p.15); cross-checked against ACT LiDAR 2019 (act-lidar-2019)
pd-osm-georef.author: project team

pd-geology-digitised.rationale: Strata thicknesses were digitised from fig.4-3 (the schematic XY cross-section of the Pëtschend horst) by colour-classifying a vertical scan of the published raster against its 20 m vertical scale bar (~6.8 px/m): Liassic package (li2 sandstone + li1 marl) ≈ 50 m at the crest, Keuper mudstone (ko) ≈ 5 m (consistent across the section), Keuper dolomitic marl (km3) ≥ 44 m (base below the section). Accuracy is ~±3 m — the figure is 'schematic' per its own caption, so these are reconstructed, not surveyed. The li2/li1 internal split is not cleanly separable on the raster; the model derives the sandstone thickness from (LiDAR surface − sandstone/marl interface) instead.
pd-geology-digitised.author: project team

pd-strata-render.rationale: The geological cross-section is a mix of sourced facts and display approximation. Sourced: the stratigraphic sequence and names (Luxembourg sandstone / marl+limestone / Keuper mudstone / dolomitic marl, fig.4-3), the ~2% SE dip (p.14), and that groundwater accumulates at the sandstone/marl interface and the gallery floor sits at that boundary (p.6, p.14, fig.5-24). Rendered faithfully: in GeoData mode the sandstone/marl interface (= groundwater table) is drawn ~2 m below the near-level gallery floor at REAL elevation, and the weathered cover uses the cited ~2 m. Now digitised (pd-geology-digitised, fig.4-3): the marl (li1 ≈ 15 m below the interface) and Keuper mudstone (ko ≈ 5 m) sub-thicknesses; km3 fills to the block base (≥44 m, base below section). Still approximate: the exact interface dip and the li2/li1 internal split (derived instead from LiDAR surface − interface). Simplification: the single groundwater line drawn is the DRAINED (post-construction) interface, rendered ~flat along the W→E gradient; the pre-construction PERCHED water table (mounded between shafts) is a separate optional overlay, and the true ~2% SE bedding dip is suppressed on the section for clarity (fig.5-03).
pd-strata-render.author: project team

pd-overflow-channel.rationale: The P-4 overflow weir and its lateral (deviation) channel are documented in plan + cross-section (fig.5-11, fig.5-14) with a Ø 80 cm channel that carries ~80 m³/d when the main channel dams above ~12 cm. The brochure gives no georeferenced route for the lateral channel, so it is modeled SCHEMATICALLY as a short branch diverging from the main gallery at P-4 (shown only when the overflow-channel option is enabled), distinct from the main channel and from the higher, unconnected auxiliary dry channel (P-7A/P-5A).
pd-overflow-channel.author: project team

pd-aux-system.rationale: P-5A and P-7A belong to a separate, higher, dry auxiliary channel ~30 m north of the main line; their channel floor sits above the aquiferous qanat channel, so they were never connected to it and are drawn as a distinct dashed channel.
pd-aux-system.author: project team

pd-werner-sit-survey.rationale: Henri Werner (brochure2018 co-author, one of the site's excavators — he sent the files but is not necessarily the original surveying party) provided a survey table ('coordonnées Raschpetzer') giving LUREF/LTM (EPSG:2169, Luxembourg's national reference system maintained by the Administration du Cadastre et de la Topographie) X/Y — and, for most points, a ground-surface Z (m a.s.l.) — for the shaft CENTREPOINT of the main line P-1 through P9 (11 points). Converted to WGS84 (pyproj, EPSG:2169→EPSG:4326) and applied to supersede the OSM/similarity-fit reconciliation (pd-osm-georef) for these 11 shafts; the western/auxiliary shafts P-4/P-5/P-5A/P-7A are NOT in this table and remain OSM-derived. COMPARISON vs. the prior OSM-derived positions: P1, P4-P9 shift <1 m (validates OSM was already accurate there); P-1 and P0 (marked '~', approximate markings, not centrepoint surveys) shift ~2.2-3.3 m; P3 (surface-depression centre, still backfilled) shifts 4.9 m; P2 (surface-depression centre) shifts 10.9 m — closely matching pd-osm-georef's own finding that 'the OSM P2 node reads ~one-third too far east'. VALIDATION: the new P2-P3 planar distance (LUREF) is 28.75 m, matching the documented 'P3 exactly on the P1-P5 line, 28 m from P2' (p.17) far more closely than the prior OSM-derived spacing (~20 m) — resolving the SSOT's long-standing spacing-invariant warning. CORRECTION NOTE: the source table's P5 X-value was transcribed without a decimal point ('78758549'); read here as 78758.549 m, the only placement consistent with the ~24-32 m shaft-to-shaft spacing established by every neighbouring leg (P3-P4=31.4 m, P4-P5=31.6 m, P5-P6=23.8 m) — flagged as an editorial correction, not a literal transcription.
pd-werner-sit-survey.basis: Henri Werner, unpublished survey table + CAD site plans, provided directly to this project (2026-07-20); cross-checked against Brochure 2018 p.17 (documented P2-P3 = 28 m) and pd-osm-georef
pd-werner-sit-survey.author: project team

pd-werner-1997-remeasurement.rationale: Henri Werner separately shared, by email to the Raschpëtzer research group (21 July 2026), a simpler 7-point coordinate table (P-7A, P-5A, P-4, P-1, P0, P1, P2) that he re-measured from a 1997 contour-survey plan — sent the day after Guy Waringo's richer werner-sit-survey table. For the four points also covered by werner-sit-survey (P-1, P0, P1, P2), the two agree within 0.3 m, corroborating that adopted survey rather than adding anything new. For P-4, P-5A, and P-7A, werner-sit-survey explicitly does not cover these western/auxiliary shafts (see that source's own note), so this re-measurement is the only non-OSM candidate position data for them: converted to WGS84 (pyproj, EPSG:2169→EPSG:4326), it shifts P-4 by ~2.4 m, P-5A by ~8.2 m, and P-7A by ~28.5 m from their current OSM-derived positions (pd-osm-georef). DECISION: not adopted. This is a single hand-measured reading transcribed from a scanned plan by one person, not a certified survey with an independent cross-check the way werner-sit-survey has (multiple overlapping points, distance-invariant validation against the brochure) — a materially weaker evidentiary basis than the bar the project has held for every other position correction so far. P-4/P-5A/P-7A therefore keep their existing osm-qanat-shafts position and geoConfidence (medium/low/low); this candidate shift is recorded in each shaft's _prov.geo note as a value worth checking against a proper survey, not a value to plot.
pd-werner-1997-remeasurement.basis: Henri Werner, 1997 contour-survey plan + coordinate table, shared by email to the Raschpëtzer research group (2026-07-21); cross-checked against werner-sit-survey and pd-osm-georef
pd-werner-1997-remeasurement.author: project team

pd-p1-sounding-gallery.rationale: The brochure documents an intermediate gallery in shaft P1 at a depth of 20 m (P1's total depth is 30 m), oriented east-west like the main gallery but with no channel, and offers two unresolved theories for it: (a) it captured a pre-qanat natural spring on the hillside, draining toward the (also unconnected) auxiliary shafts P-5A/P-7A, before the whole system was later lowered ~10 m to its present depth; or (b) P1 was sunk first, and this gallery is an exploratory 'sounding' tunnel dug to locate the water-bearing strata before the final route/depth was set from the completed 'mother shaft' P9 — in which case it was abandoned once its purpose was served. The brochure presents both without resolving between them. Its horizontal extent was never surveyed — fig 5-3 marks it with '?' on both sides — so the model draws a short SCHEMATIC stub (6 m) centred on P1, not a measured tunnel; shown only when the option is enabled.
pd-p1-sounding-gallery.author: project team
